titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْوَرَ أُصِيبَتْ عَيْنُهُ الصَّحِيحَةُ فَفُقِئَتْ أَنْ تُفْقَأَ إِحْدَى عَيْنَيْ صَاحِبِهِ وَ يُعْقَلَ لَهُ نِصْفُ الدِّيَةِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ أَخَذَ دِيَةً كَامِلَةً وَ يُعْفَى عَنْ عَيْنِ صَاحِبِهِ .
arabic.text: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْوَرَ أُصِيبَتْ عَيْنُهُ الصَّحِيحَةُ فَفُقِئَتْ أَنْ تُفْقَأَ إِحْدَى عَيْنَيْ صَاحِبِهِ وَ يُعْقَلَ لَهُ نِصْفُ الدِّيَةِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ أَخَذَ دِيَةً كَامِلَةً وَ يُعْفَى عَنْ عَيْنِ صَاحِبِهِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya,f rom Ahmad Bin Muhammad, both together from Ibn Abu Najran, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Muhammad Bin Qays who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding a one-eyed man whose correct eye was hit, so it was blinded: ‘He can blind an eye of his companions and pay him half the wergild, and if he so desires to, he can take the complete wergild, and forgive an eye of his companion’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya,f rom Ahmad Bin Muhammad, both together from Ibn Abu Najran, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Muhammad Bin Qays who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding a one-eyed man whose correct eye was hit, so it was blinded: ‘He can blind an eye of his companions and pay him half the wergild, and if he so desires to, he can take the complete wergild, and forgive an eye of his companion’.

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ جَمِيعاً عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجْرَانَ عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: فِي عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ الدِّيَةُ .
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,
Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> has said: ‘With regards to an eye of the one-eyed, is the (complete) wergild’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ali Bin Abu Hamza, from Abu Baseer,

Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> has said: ‘With regards to an eye of the one-eyed, is the (complete) wergild’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: فِي عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ الدِّيَةُ كَامِلَةً .
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘With regards to an eye of the one-eyed, is the complete wergild’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘With regards to an eye of the one-eyed, is the complete wergild’.

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَمَّادٍ عَنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ يَدَ رَجُلٍ شَلَّاءَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ .
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ يَدَ رَجُلٍ شَلَّاءَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Hammad Bin Ziyad, from Suleyman Bin Khalid –
With regards to a man whose paralysed hand was cut by a man. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him (the cutter) is a third of the wergild’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Hammad Bin Ziyad, from Suleyman Bin Khalid –

With regards to a man whose paralysed hand was cut by a man. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him (the cutter) is a third of the wergild’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ فِي

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: ] فِي الْعَيْنِ الْعَوْرَاءِ تَكُونُ قَائِمَةً فَتُخْسَفُ فَقَالَ قَضَى فِيهَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام ) نِصْفَ الدِّيَةِ فِي الْعَيْنِ الصَّحِيحَةِ .
arabic.text: ] فِي الْعَيْنِ الْعَوْرَاءِ تَكُونُ قَائِمَةً فَتُخْسَفُ فَقَالَ قَضَى فِيهَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام ) نِصْفَ الدِّيَةِ فِي الْعَيْنِ الصَّحِيحَةِ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Hameed, from Abu Jameela, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman, from Abdullah Bin Abu Ja’far,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding the eye of the one-eyed which was firm, then it blurred out. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup> had judged with regards to it being half the wergild for the healthy eye’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Musa Bin Al Hassan, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Hameed, from Abu Jameela, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman, from Abdullah Bin Abu Ja’far,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding the eye of the one-eyed which was firm, then it blurred out. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup> had judged with regards to it being half the wergild for the healthy eye’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) [أَنَّهُ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: فِي لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ ذَكَرِ الْخَصِيِّ وَ أُنْثَيَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ .
arabic.text: فِي لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ ذَكَرِ الْخَصِيِّ وَ أُنْثَيَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazaz, from Bureyd Bin Muawiya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘With regards to a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the blind, and one of castrated manhood and his testicles, is a third of the wergild’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazaz, from Bureyd Bin Muawiya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘With regards to a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the blind, and one of castrated manhood and his testicles, is a third of the wergild’.

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ الْخَزَّازِ عَنْ بُرَيْدِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: سَأَلَهُ بَعْضُ آلِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ لِسَانَ رَجُلٍ أَخْرَسَ [قَالَ] فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَ هُوَ أَخْرَسُ فَعَلَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لِسَانُهُ ذَهَبَ بِهِ وَجَعٌ أَوْ آفَةٌ بَعْدَ مَا كَانَ يَتَكَلَّمُ فَإِنَّ عَلَى الَّذِي قَطَعَ لِسَانَهُ ثُلُثَ دِيَةِ لِسَانِهِ قَالَ وَ كَذَلِكَ الْقَضَاءُ فِي الْعَيْنَيْنِ وَ الْجَوَارِحِ
قَالَ هَكَذَا وَجَدْنَاهُ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) .
arabic.text:
سَأَلَهُ بَعْضُ آلِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ لِسَانَ رَجُلٍ أَخْرَسَ [قَالَ] فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَ هُوَ أَخْرَسُ فَعَلَيْهِ ثُلُثُ الدِّيَةِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لِسَانُهُ ذَهَبَ بِهِ وَجَعٌ أَوْ آفَةٌ بَعْدَ مَا كَانَ يَتَكَلَّمُ فَإِنَّ عَلَى الَّذِي قَطَعَ لِسَانَهُ ثُلُثَ دِيَةِ لِسَانِهِ قَالَ وَ كَذَلِكَ الْقَضَاءُ فِي الْعَيْنَيْنِ وَ الْجَوَارِحِ

قَالَ هَكَذَا وَجَدْنَاهُ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, both together from Ibn Mahboub, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘One of the progeny of Zurara asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who cut the tongue of a mute man. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If it was such that his mother gave birth to him and he was mute, so upon him is a third of the wergild; and if it was such that his tongue went with away by illness or affliction after him having spoken beforehand, so upon the one who cut his tongue is a third of the wergild of his tongue; and similar to that is the judgement regarding the two eyes, and the limbs’.
He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘This is how we<sup>asws</sup> found it to be in the Book of Ali<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, and Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, both together from Ibn Mahboub, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘One of the progeny of Zurara asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who cut the tongue of a mute man. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If it was such that his mother gave birth to him and he was mute, so upon him is a third of the wergild; and if it was such that his tongue went with away by illness or affliction after him having spoken beforehand, so upon the one who cut his tongue is a third of the wergild of his tongue; and similar to that is the judgement regarding the two eyes, and the limbs’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘This is how we<sup>asws</sup> found it to be in the Book of Ali<sup>asws</sup>’.

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ جَمِيعاً عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب دِيَةِ عَيْنِ الْأَعْمَى وَ يَدِ الْأَشَلِّ وَ لِسَانِ الْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ الْأَعْوَرِ
titles.en: Chapter 29 – Wergild for the eye of the blind, and a hand of the paralytic, and a tongue of the mute, and an eye of the one-eyed

segments: رَجُلٍ فَقَأَ عَيْنَ رَجُلٍ ذَاهِبَةً وَ هِيَ قَائِمَةٌ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ رُبُعُ دِيَةِ الْعَيْنِ .
arabic.text: رَجُلٍ فَقَأَ عَيْنَ رَجُلٍ ذَاهِبَةً وَ هِيَ قَائِمَةٌ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ رُبُعُ دِيَةِ الْعَيْنِ .

segments: Ali, from his father, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Abu Jameela, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, regarding a man who blinded an eye of a man, (whose vision) had gone, and it (the eye) was firm’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him is the quarter of the wergild of an eye’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali, from his father, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Abu Jameela, from Mufazzal Bin Salih, from Abdullah Bin Suleyman,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, regarding a man who blinded an eye of a man, (whose vision) had gone, and it (the eye) was firm’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him is the quarter of the wergild of an eye’.

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيٌّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنْ مُفَضَّلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ
